name: Peanut Butter Cup Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: Golden brown sugar, firmly packed: 2 tbsp
Golden brown sugar, firmly packed: 1 1/2 cups
Peanut butter, creamy: 1/2 cup (do not use freshly-ground)
Reese's Peanut Butter Cups: 10 oz (cut into 3/4-inch pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F and position rack in the center.
2. Butter a 9-inch-diameter springform pan with 2-3/4-inch-high sides.
3. Mix chocolate cookie crumbs, chopped peanuts, melted butter, brown sugar, and salt until well combined. Press mixture evenly onto the bottom and half-inch up the sides of the pan. Bake until crust is set, about 8 minutes. Cool on a rack.
4. Reduce oven temperature to 325°F.
5. In a large bowl, beat cream cheese and brown sugar until smooth. Add peanut butter and vanilla extract; beat just until blended.
6. Add eggs one at a time, beating just until blended after each addition. Add whipping cream and beat until smooth. Stir in peanut butter cup pieces.
7. Pour filling into the crust. Bake until sides of cake are set but center still moves slightly, about 55 minutes. Cool on a rack for 10 minutes.
8. Blend sour cream, sugar, and vanilla extract in a medium bowl. Carefully spoon topping over cheesecake. Return to oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes.
9. Let cool in the pan on a rack. Run a knife around the edge of the cheesecake to loosen it. Cover and refrigerate overnight (or up to 3 days). Release pan sides, let stand at room temperature for 20 minutes before serving.
